title: Authoring a Spinner
summary: The SI walks the Loom through producing a working Spinner end-to-end. The patron watches: thirteen narrated steps, every one driven by the SI itself — typing, clicking, submitting. No patron input required. At each step the SI tells you what it's about to do, does it, and verifies. On error it remediates and re-runs. Your job is to witness and, at the end, say whether what you saw delighted or fell short.

fixtures.description: First Spinner authored through Weaver's Tension — proof that a patron can sit at the Loom in a browser and produce a working Spinner end-to-end without writing a line of code.

steps.title: Open the Loom
steps.narration: Opening the Loom — the Cell's authoring surface. Every Spinner originates here. The page should show the Cell's status, the nav on the left, and a heading that names where we are.

steps.title: Glance at recent operations
steps.narration: Moving to the operations log. Every multi-step task the Loom performs on a patron's behalf lands here as an envelope — input, output, timing, the audit events it produced. By the end of this run, two new envelopes will be visible: one for the install, one for the invocation.

steps.title: The Skein
steps.narration: The Skein — the catalogue of every Spinner installed in this Cell. Each row carries its signers, its recorded digest, and an integrity status. 'Verified' means the bytes on disk still match what was signed. Trust isn't on faith here; it's on a fresh check.

steps.title: Open the authoring form
steps.narration: Clicking the New Spinner affordance — the door to authoring.

steps.title: Select the hello-spinner template
steps.narration: Picking the hello-spinner template — the only template in this Cell. A minimal Spinner with one capability, greet, that returns a greeting. Templates are the architecture's apprenticeship: every new Spinner inherits its bones from a precedent.

steps.title: Fill the slug
steps.narration: Typing the slug — '{{fixture.slug}}'. The slug is the URL-safe identifier; kebab-case, lowercase, no spaces. It becomes the directory name and the URL segment. Cannot be changed later.

steps.title: Fill display name and description
steps.narration: Filling display name — '{{fixture.displayName}}' — and the description. The display name shows in the Skein listing; the description is one paragraph about what this Spinner does.

steps.title: Submit — the install runs
steps.narration: Submitting the authoring form. The Loom scaffolds the bundle from the template, lints it, signs it with this Cell's identity key, records the digest, and writes a Skein row with status 'verified'. About five seconds, all in.

onError.narration: The install didn't land in the Skein. Reloading the detail page once — sometimes the redirect arrives a beat before the row is committed.

steps.title: The detail page
steps.narration: Landing on the Spinner's detail page — its manifest at top, signers + digest recorded, capabilities listed below. The Refresh integrity button is always live: click it any time and the Loom rereads the bytes from disk and recomputes the digest. Trust without faith.

steps.title: Invoke the greet capability
steps.narration: Typing { "name": "World" } into the capability input, then clicking Run. The Weaver resolves the Spinner's entrypoint, loads it through dispatch, calls greet, captures the output, and writes a wp.spinner.invoke audit event. This is the first time a Spinner authored in this run actually runs in this Cell.

steps.title: Return to the Skein
steps.narration: Back at the Skein listing. The newly-authored Spinner should appear with a 'verified' badge.

steps.title: Operations — install + invoke envelopes
steps.narration: Back at the operations log. Two envelopes should be present since the start: spinner.install and the invocation. Each one's detail surfaces its input, output, and the audit events it correlated to.

steps.title: Audit log — the install event
steps.narration: And the audit log. The wp.spinner.install event row carries the manifest, the digest, the signers, the actor. Everything an auditor needs to reconstruct what happened without any prior trust. The Foundation Pledge in concrete form: every action observable forever.

steps.title: One more thing — Refresh integrity
steps.narration: Back on the detail page. Clicking Refresh integrity rereads the bundle, recomputes the digest, compares it to what was recorded at install. The badge stays 'verified' because nothing's been tampered with. A wp.spinner.integrity-checked event lands. The trust loop closes: verified at install, re-verifiable on demand forever.
